description:
EyeSpace is a specialised medical technology company at the forefront of contact lens development and design. We support eye care practitioners in engineering custom-made contact lenses through our advanced design software platform, streamlining the full workflow from design to production. Our platform comprehensively supports lens design, order management, and collaboration with laboratories.

Our software development, one of the core operation teams of EyeSpace comprises of individuals of varying IT background and with decades of industry experience.

Core Job Duty includes:

·       Maintain web-based frontend applications supporting optometry workflows by ensuring program code meets system requirements, software designs, and technical specifications in accordance with quality standards

·       Participating in technical discussions and code reviews, identifying limitations and opportunities for improvement in existing software and associated processes

·       Testing, debugging, diagnosing and correcting errors and faults in an applications programming language within established testing protocols, guidelines and quality standards to ensure programs and applications perform to specification

·       Developing software/platform by updating technical program, end user documentation and operational procedures

·       Integrating and maintain the desktop and online systems

·       Providing advice, guidance and expertise in developing proposals and strategies for software design activities such as financial evaluation and costings for recommending software purchases and upgrades

·       Collaborating with designers, optometrists, and laboratory teams to implement clinical and manufacturing requirements

·       Supporting production systems in clinical and lab environments and travelling internationally to coordinate with laboratories if required.

 

Skills/Experience/Qualifications Required

·      3 years experience in web and desktop software development, including backend development with Java and SQL, and strong experience with Svelte, SvelteKit, and TypeScript

·      Tertiary qualifications in an ICT-related major

·      Experience in healthcare and/or optometry-related systems will be ideal

·      Demonstrated experience working within B2B and B2C environments is preferable

·      Strong verbal and written communication skills to liaise with internal and external stakeholders, Chinese and English bilingual speaker is preferable

·      Ideally familiar with the Chinese clients and be able to communicate with the suppliers in China

·      Excellent team worker, and be able to work between members from different cultural background

 

Applicants must have full Australian working rights.
